source: Philippine Vernacular, Bay & Ong, Contemporary Practice

rules.description: Minimum elevation of habitable floor above grade for ventilation
rules.notes: 600mm allows meaningful under-floor airflow. Traditional bahay kubo: 1.0-2.0m. Higher elevation = better flood protection + ventilation + pest barrier.

rules.description: Under-floor space should be open for air circulation, not enclosed
rules.notes: Enclosing the under-floor space traps moisture, creates mold, and eliminates the cooling benefit. If enclosed for storage, provide vent openings on all sides.

rules.description: Stilt/pier material must resist ground moisture and termites
rules.notes: Concrete piers with termite shields preferred. Hardwood posts (molave, ipil) with soil-contact treatment. Never use untreated softwood in ground contact.

rules.description: Termite barrier required between ground contact and structure
rules.notes: Physical barrier (stainless steel mesh, granite particle layer) or chemical barrier at foundation-to-structure junction. Tropics have year-round termite pressure.

rules.description: Covered outdoor living area as percentage of total floor area
rules.notes: Traditional Filipino home: 30-50% of usable space is semi-outdoor (verandah, terrace, lanai). Covered but open-sided for breeze. Extends usable living space year-round.

rules.description: Transition zone (covered porch/verandah) between outdoor and indoor
rules.notes: Minimum 1.8m deep covered transition allows eyes to adjust from bright sun, shoes to be removed, and rain to be shed before entering. Traditional: 2.4-3.0m.

rules.description: Floor level change between outdoor deck and indoor rooms
rules.notes: 50-150mm step-up from outdoor deck to interior prevents rain splash and wind-driven water ingress. Also provides psychological threshold between spaces.

rules.description: Operable walls/sliding panels for flexible indoor-outdoor connection
rules.notes: Full-width sliding or folding panels allow the living space to open completely to the outdoors in fair weather and close during storms. Traditional capiz shell sliding panels (ventanilla) serve this function.

rules.description: Adjustable louvers preferred over fixed windows for tropical climate
rules.notes: Louvers allow ventilation during rain (angled up), full airflow in dry weather (horizontal), and closure during typhoons. Jalousie windows are the tropical standard.

rules.description: Mosquito screening on all operable openings
rules.notes: Essential in dengue/malaria zones. Stainless steel or fiberglass mesh (18x16 mesh count). Must not significantly reduce airflow — specify low-profile mesh.

rules.description: Detachable typhoon shutters for all windows and openings
rules.notes: Rolling aluminum shutters, folding steel shutters, or pre-fitted plywood covers. Must be operable by one person. Store on-site when not deployed.
